query_normalized: investigating the impact of network centrality on resting-state functional connectivity in autism spectrum disorder neuroscience
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (e) as an empirical-population canonical study because it analyzes resting-state fMRI data specifically from the Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) population using the ABIDE dataset, which is the standard resource for this domain. Additionally, it addresses criterion (b) by providing the foundational resting-state network (RSN) data and connectivity patterns necessary to investigate how network properties (like centrality) differ in ASD, even though it focuses on the
